headline: 29% target completion with strong KF expansion demand; geographic concentration risk emerging.
collection_status: 393 of 1,350 responses (29%) collected. Lindi and Mara dominate with 134 responses (34% of total). Pace requires acceleration: at current rate, full target not achievable without intensified regional push in underperforming zones.
key_findings: KF expansion is dominant priority (139/393, 35% of sample) with new programs (92) and policy integration (83) as secondary drivers—stakeholders want scale, not consolidation.
Teacher respondents represent 85% of sample (211/393), heavily weighted toward KF lower grades (153). Head teacher engagement strong but DEO/REO/REC participation critically thin (15 combined).
Rukwa (9), Singida (25), and Songwe (29) severely underrepresented; Rukwa at 2.7% of target. Geographic skew risks biased priority data and weak district-level buy-in.
Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) but scale readiness low (2.7/5)—stakeholders want expansion but doubt organizational/system capacity to deliver.
flags: District and regional official engagement critically weak (15 respondents across DEO, REC, REC, REO roles). Without these gatekeepers, policy and implementation risk remains high.
Rukwa, Singida, and Songwe combined represent only 63 responses (16% of current total). Must surge collection in these three regions or risk regional validity and representation.
Low scale readiness (2.7/5) against high expansion demand signals potential disconnect between stakeholder ambitions and program readiness—risk of over-commitment.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection teams to Rukwa (priority 1), Singida, and Songwe with explicit DEO/REC quotas. Simultaneously, conduct rapid capacity assessment against expansion priorities to bridge 4.4/2.7 satisfaction-readiness gap before scaling commitments.
